frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: downstream processes integrate sensory pulses to drive perceptual choices

frameworks.name: sensory-cognitive interaction in cortex
frameworks.one_line: even early sensory cortex carries choice and accumulated-evidence signals

frameworks.name: veridical sensory encoding
frameworks.one_line: view that sensory cortices pass noisy but unmodulated inputs forward

core_question: How does posterior cortex internally represent sequential sensory pulses that jointly drive an accumulation-based decision?
key_assumptions: decision is built from temporally sparse, randomly timed sensory pulses
cue-locked single units in posterior cortex index the per-pulse sensory response
amplitude of cue-locked responses can be modulated by task variables
manipulations: randomly timed pulses of visual evidence during navigation
varied upcoming behavioral choice across trials
recording across multiple posterior cortical areas including V1
key_findings: only a small fraction of active neurons in V1 had sensory-like cue-locked responses
cue-locked neurons showed amplitude modulations ranging from sensory to cognitive
amplitudes were modulated by choice and by accumulated evidence
task-related modulations spanned a large fraction of cue-locked neurons across posterior cortex
interpretations: early sensory cortices do not provide purely veridical inputs to accumulation
future models of accumulation behavior should incorporate sensory-cortical task modulations
